decision_summary: The chip temperature (89.2°C) exceeds the 85.0°C limit. Since material tuning alone has stalled, I will implement a coordinated geometry and material change: increase the heat spreader width to capture more lateral area and simultaneously increase its conductivity to maximize spreading efficiency. This addresses the high-severity violation by reducing thermal resistance.

changes.reason: Widening the spreader increases the cross-sectional area for lateral heat flow, moving heat away from the chip center towards the cooled boundaries.

Conductivity is already at the upper bound (500.0 W/mK). No change possible here, but retaining it in the plan confirms we are leveraging the maximum available material property while relying on geometry for further gains.

expected_effects: Reduction in chip peak temperature below 85.0°C
More uniform temperature distribution across the spreader
Improved heat flux towards the left and right Dirichlet boundaries
risk_notes: Spreader width is being pushed to the domain limit (1.0m); ensure no overlap violations occur with boundary conditions in future steps.
Diminishing returns may occur if the spreader becomes too wide relative to the heat source location.
Material conductivity cannot be increased further as it is already at the maximum bound.
